title: Tutoring Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Tutoring Services 36 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Autism Spectrum Disorder Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION MULTI-COUNTY OFFICE UPLIFT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tutoring and academic support by helping youth and families navigate complex academic systems. Also provides leadership training, culturally-specific mentorship, and athletics. 2110 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 9-26 who are immigrants or refugees Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Email (971) 254-8916 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE UPLIFT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tutoring and academic support by helping youth and families navigate complex academic systems. Also provides leadership training, culturally-specific mentorship, and athletics. 6835 SW Clinton Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Ages 9-26 who are immigrants or refugees Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Email (971) 254-8916 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ANTFARM AXIS LEARNING CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a youth education center with academic and youth support services. Provides 13 computers to create, facilitate learning, and access to information. Assisting young people with completion of high school, establishment of educational goals, and movement towards college and/or work readiness are core services offered. Community members offer support through tutoring, mentoring, community volunteering, job-skills training, supported employment, and assisting young people with problem solving and critical thinking skills. 39140 Proctor Boulevard Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 668-9955 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLOSSER CENTER FOR DYSLEXIA RESOURCES ORTON-GILLINGHAM AND LAUNCH TUTORING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one or small group reading, spelling, writing and math tutoring services using individualized techniques. 1907 NE 45th Avenue Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Grades 1-12 who have learning disabilities. Partial scholarships are available. Hours: Varies by class Email (503) 234-4060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF CORVALLIS BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 1112 NW Circle Boulevard Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Ages 5-18 Hours: Hours vary by activity. Regular after-school programming Monday-Friday after school until 6pm. Email (541) 757-1909 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF EMERALD VALLEY BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive space for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 1545 W 22nd Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5:30pm Email (541) 345-9939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive and safe place for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 203 SE 9th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Grades K-8 Hours: After school hours: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-6pm Summer and Non-School Day Hours: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5:15pm Email (541) 479-5258 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY ILLINOIS VALLEY BOYS & GIRLS CLUB BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Boys and Girls Club provides a positive and safe place for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, that help them achieve academic success, healthy lifestyles, and good character and citizenship. After-school and summer programs for youth grades K-8. 24535 Redwood Highway Kerby, OR 97531 Eligibility: Grades K-8 Hours: After school hours: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-6pm Summer and Non-School Day Hours: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5:15pm Email (541) 244-8490 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY PHOENIX BOYS & GIRLS CLUB BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive and safe place for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 215 North Rose Street Phoenix, OR 97535 Eligibility: Grades K-8 Hours: After school hours: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-6pm Summer and Non-School Day Hours: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5:15pm Email (541) 218-5940 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF THE ROGUE VALLEY TALENT BOYS & GIRLS CLUB BOYS & GIRLS CLUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a positive and safe place for youth to engage in fun and empowering programs, such as leadership and community service, after-school programs, academic enrichment, workforce training, performing and visual arts, camps, and personal health and fitness. 307 W Wagner Creek Road Room 14 Talent, OR 97540 Eligibility: Grades K-8 Hours: After school hours: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-6pm Summer and Non-School Day Hours: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5:15pm Email (541) 292-4851 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGEWAY HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers developmental therapies and supports including in-home occupational and speech therapy, social groups, classes, and tutoring. Also offers parent support groups, parent and child activity groups, parenting classes, and specialized information and referral. 555 E 15th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families with children living with autism and related disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 345-0805 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CANBY CENTER EDUCATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides after school tutoring, reading mentors, and finance, cooking, and sewing life skills classes. Eligibility: Grades K-12 for after school tutoring, unrestricted for all other programs Hours: Varies by class Email (503) 266-2920 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! MCMINNVILLE SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 117 NE 5th Street Suite H McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment Email (503) 883-9768 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEHALEM YOUTH & FAMILY SERVICES YOOP! NEWBERG SKILL CENTER YOUTH OPPORTUNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational and vocational opportunities for youth and young adults. Services include pre-employment training, Rethinking Careers Workshops, GED preparation classes, college application assistance, student financial aid assistance. Offers assistance in obtaining documentation such as ID card, Social Security card, and birth certificate. Parenting classes available for youth who have children. Child care is not available at skill centers, but vouchers may be available. 100 E 5th Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Ages 16-24. Must meet income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 554-1461 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IFOSTER FOSTER YOUTH RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Online resource for foster and former foster youth to access programs that help provide various services PO Box 159 Truckee, CA 96160 Eligibility: Current and former foster children ages 16-24 (older ages may be accepted). Current and former kinship youth, and crossover youth Hours: Not applicable Email (855) 936-7837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION YOUTH ACADEMIC SUPPORT AND PARENT ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Culturally and linguistically specific after-school activities, tutoring, youth academic advocacy, and parent engagement and education. Engages parents in group activities and provides in-home tutoring to support students' academic achievement. 10301 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6560 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO AFRICA HOUSE YOUTH ACADEMIC SUPPORT AND PARENT ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Culturally and linguistically specific after-school activities, tutoring, youth academic advocacy, and parent engagement and education. Engages parents in group activities and provides in-home tutoring to support students' academic achievement. 709 NE 102nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (971) 271-6560 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMMIGRANT AND REFUGEE COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION IRCO PACIFIC ISLANDER AND ASIAN FAMILY CENTER YOUTH ACADEMIC SUPPORT AND PARENT ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Culturally and linguistically specific after-school activities, tutoring, youth academic advocacy, and parent engagement and education. Engages parents in group activities and provides in-home tutoring to support students' academic achievement. 8040 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 200 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (971) 271-6560 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support that includes activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading club, gender groups, and conflict resolution classes; case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-17 who are at risk of academic failure Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 867-2188 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT CHIFIN NATIVE YOUTH CENTER INDIAN EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers learning, leadership, and cultural activities that engage Native youth and support a positive cultural identity. Provides culturally appropriate educational support to Native students, including tutoring and credit recovery, and supports Native families’ involvement in students’ education. 1084 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Native American and Alaskan Native students and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 726-4301 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA AMISTAD POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 30 S Gun Club Road Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA LIBERTAD POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 2921 Saddle Club Street SE #1009 Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER NORTHGATE CENTER POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides youth with mentoring, tutoring, and case management to help them graduate, find stable employment, and housing. Offers after-school programs, volunteer opportunities, and leadership development. 3850 Portland Road NE Suite 130 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Strengthening Families: Ages 10-14 and their parents Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 363-1895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading clubs, gender groups, conflict resolution classes, case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 1700 NW Civic Drive Suite 200 Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Grades 5-12 who are at risk of academic failure and their families Hours: Varies Email (971) 506-2609 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY HOMEWORK CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Includes an online research database with information on various topics. Provides live homework help through tutor.com for paper proofreading, math assistance, PSAT practice questions, and study guides. Also offers live tutoring in Spanish as a foreign language. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Live one-on-one help: -7 days per week noon-midnight in English and Spanish -7 days per week 3pm-9pm in Vietnamese Email (503) 988-5123 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NAACP EUGENE-SPRINGFIELD EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tutoring, mentoring, college preparation, and academic and social support to students. Offers a Youth Council program to connect and motivate students toward collective community involvement, cultural pride, and strong cross-cultural relationships. 330 High Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Students K-12, with emphasis on communities of color and households who qualify for free and reduced lunches. Parents are required to participate in Parent Council once per month. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (541) 484-1119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an advocacy program that helps students and their families set and achieve goals in four key areas: Mind, Body, Spirit, and Context. Services include wrap-around services, advocacy services, college visits, life skills workshops, job training, financial aid assistance, school tutoring, after-school homework help, and the development of cultural pride and relevant skills. Incentives like stipends, meals, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and financial assistance for youth participants. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Portland Public School District: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth -Successful Families 2020: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within David Douglas and Reynolds school districts. -SUN Youth Advocacy: 5th-10th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within Centennial, David Douglas, Gresham-Barlow, Parkrose, Portland Public, and Reynolds school districts. OR Region 3: 5th-10th grade any race/ethnicity enrolled in the following schools: Alice Ott Middle, Cherry Park Elementary, Gilbert Heights Elementary, Gilbert Park Elementary, Parkrose High, Parkrose Middle, Prescott Elementary, Sacramento Elementary, and Shaver Elementary Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm, some evening hours for events Email (503) 288-8177 329 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER EDUCATION AND EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers GED preparation and testing, GED/high school credit recovery and tutoring that is Portland Public School accredited, work readiness training, job search help, resume help, college preparation, and help with post-secondary education and training. "Urban Ed" is a drop-in, student-centered alternative school for youth who have dropped out or are at risk of dropping out of school. Work experiences are available at the Virginia Woof (VW) Dog Daycare and Youth Job Training Program. Child care is also available for established clients. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are low-income, homeless, or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm and 2pm-5pm, Wednesday/Friday 10am-1pm Email (503) 445-0976 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX PARENT PDXPARENT.COM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about parent support groups, children's playgroups, and family activities. Offers a community events calendar, and magazine as well. Website Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 460-2774 Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM MEDFORD LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that provides adult English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction and adult basic education for parents. Children participate in the program with their parents by attending developmentally appropriate classes and getting homework help and Spanish reading support. 1001 Beall Lane Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-15 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 5:30pm-8pm (541) 734-5150 1049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA VALLEY DISABILITIES NETWORK PROJECT LITERACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Tutoring and adult literacy program that offers one-on-one and small group instruction in reading, writing, GED preparation, and English as a Second Language (ESL) courses. 736 SE Jackson Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities and adults who identify as having low literacy skills Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 672-6336 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY COOPERATIVE LIBRARY SERVICES BRAINFUSE HELPNOW Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides study and practice tests for AP exams, SAT, American College Testing (ACT), General Educational Development (GED), Armed Services Vocational Aptitude Battery (ASVAB), and US Citizenship. Also provides assistance with writing assessments and provides tutoring for math, reading, writing, science, and social studies. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Washington County library card holder Writing assistance and tutoring: K-12 students Hours: All other services: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Tutor: 7 days per week 1pm-10pm, closed holidays Email (503) 846-3222 Visit Website More Details WINSTON AREA COMMUNITY PARTNERSHIP WINSTON TEEN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supervised out-of-school-time youth center where teens can participate in academic enrichment activities, including the Teen Cuisine culinary program, a learning garden, Science Action Club, a theatre/drama club, and the Youth Entrepreneur Success youth-run business. Also provides homework assistance, computers, board games, game consoles, and a pool table. A daily meal is provided through the USDA food program. Art program is available during the summer. 440 SE Grape Avenue Winston, OR 97496 Eligibility: Grades 6-12 or ages 11-18 Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 2:30pm-5:30pm, Friday 2:30pm-5:00pm (closed major holidays) Spring Break and Summer: Monday-Friday 2:30pm-5pm Email (541) 671-1274 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP YOUTH OUTREACH SERVICES YOUTH
